questions.stem: In Microsoft Excel, what is the name of the single file that contains one or more worksheets?
questions.explanation: An Excel file is called a workbook, and it holds one or more worksheets (the individual tabbed pages). A cell is a single box, a range is a group of cells, and the formula bar is where cell contents are shown and edited. The whole saved file is the workbook.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: What is the intersection of a single row and a single column in a worksheet called?
questions.explanation: A cell is the basic unit of a worksheet, formed where one column crosses one row. The workbook is the whole file, a tab selects a worksheet, and a chart is a graphical object. Data is entered into cells.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: How are columns labelled in an Excel worksheet?
options: With Roman numerals such as I, II, III
With letters such as A, B, C
questions.explanation: Columns run vertically and are labelled with letters (A, B, C and on to AA, AB and so on). Rows run horizontally and are the ones labelled with numbers. So a cell address combines a column letter with a row number.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: How are rows labelled in an Excel worksheet?
options: With letters such as A, B, C
questions.explanation: Rows run horizontally and are labelled with numbers starting at 1. Columns are the ones labelled with letters. The cell where column B meets row 3 is therefore addressed as B3.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Which of the following is a valid cell address (cell reference) in Excel?
questions.explanation: A cell address is written as the column letter first, then the row number, with no space or separator, so C5 means column C, row 5. Writing the number first (5C), inserting a hyphen (C-5), or spelling it out are all invalid forms.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: The cell where column D meets row 8 has which address?
questions.explanation: Cell addresses put the column letter before the row number, so column D and row 8 give D8. The other forms reverse the order, replace the row with a letter, or use coordinates that Excel does not use for ordinary references.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Which column comes immediately after column Z in an Excel worksheet?
questions.explanation: After the single letters A through Z, Excel continues with two-letter labels beginning at AA, then AB, AC and so on up to AZ, then BA. So the column right after Z is AA. This lets the worksheet have many more than 26 columns.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: A group of selected cells, such as the block from A1 to C3, is called what in Excel?
questions.explanation: A range is any rectangular block of cells treated as a unit, and it is written with the top-left and bottom-right corners separated by a colon, as in A1:C3. A workbook is the whole file, a macro is recorded automation, and a header is repeating text at the top of a printed page.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: How is the range covering every cell from B2 down and across to D5 written in Excel?
questions.explanation: A range uses a colon between the first and last cell, so the block from B2 to D5 is written B2:D5. A hyphen would be read as subtraction, a comma lists separate cells rather than a block, and the word to is not valid range syntax.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: You select cells from A1 to A10 in a single column. How many cells are in this range?
questions.explanation: The range A1:A10 runs down one column from row 1 to row 10, which is 10 - 1 + 1 = 10 cells. Forgetting that both endpoints are included would give 9, and 100 would only be right for a 10 by 10 block, not a single column.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: The range A1:C4 forms a rectangular block. How many cells does it contain?
questions.explanation: The block spans columns A, B and C (3 columns) and rows 1 to 4 (4 rows), so it contains 3 times 4 = 12 cells. Adding the counts instead of multiplying would wrongly give 7.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: By default, when you type a number into a cell, how does Excel align it?
options: To the right of the cell
In the centre of the cell
To the left of the cell
Numbers cannot be typed into cells
questions.explanation: Excel right-aligns numbers (and dates) by default, while text is left-aligned by default. This default alignment is a quick visual clue: if a value you expect to be a number sits on the left, Excel may be treating it as text.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: By default, when you type ordinary text (such as a name) into a cell, how does Excel align it?
options: To the right of the cell
To the left of the cell
questions.explanation: Text is left-aligned by default, whereas numbers and dates are right-aligned by default. Because of this, a number accidentally stored as text will appear on the left and will not behave correctly in calculations.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: You type the digits 007 into a plain General-format cell and press Enter. What does Excel normally display in the cell?
options: The number 7 with the leading zeros removed
questions.explanation: Excel reads 007 as the number seven and drops the leading zeros, showing 7. To keep the leading zeros (for an ID or phone number) you must format the cell as Text first, or type a leading apostrophe, so Excel stores the entry as text rather than a number.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Which key do you press to confirm an entry and move the active cell down to the next row?
questions.explanation: Pressing Enter accepts what you typed and normally moves the selection down one cell. Esc cancels the entry, Delete clears cell contents, and F1 opens Help. Tab is the key that confirms and moves to the right instead.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: While typing in a cell, which key cancels the entry and leaves the cell with its previous contents?
questions.explanation: Esc cancels the current entry before it is committed, restoring whatever the cell held before. Enter and Tab both confirm the entry, and the spacebar just inserts a space into what you are typing.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: The strip near the top of the Excel window that shows and lets you edit the contents of the active cell is called what?
questions.explanation: The formula bar displays the true contents of the selected cell, including any underlying formula, and is where you can edit it. The status bar at the bottom shows quick totals and mode indicators, the title bar shows the file name, and scroll bars move the view.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: The small box to the left of the formula bar that displays the address of the active cell (and lets you type an address to jump to) is called what?
questions.explanation: The Name Box, at the left end of the formula bar, shows the active cell address such as B3 and lets you type a cell address or range name to navigate there. The Ribbon holds command tabs, the Quick Access Toolbar holds shortcut buttons, and sheet tabs switch worksheets.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: The tabs at the bottom of the Excel window that you click to switch between Sheet1, Sheet2 and so on are called what?
questions.explanation: Sheet tabs sit along the bottom and let you move between the worksheets in a workbook. Ribbon tabs (such as Home and Insert) are at the top and switch command groups, while a dialog launcher opens a settings box and a fill handle is the small square used to copy cells.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: The small square at the bottom-right corner of a selected cell, which you drag to copy or extend a series, is called what?
questions.explanation: The fill handle is the tiny square at the lower-right of the selection. Dragging it copies the cell or continues a pattern such as Jan, Feb, Mar. The Name Box shows an address, the split bar divides the window into panes, and the formula bar edits contents.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: You enter 5 in A1 and 10 in A2, select both cells, then drag the fill handle down to A5. What value usually appears in A5?
questions.explanation: Excel detects the step of +5 between 5 and 10 and continues the series: A3 becomes 15, A4 becomes 20 and A5 becomes 25. Dragging a recognised linear pattern extends it rather than just repeating the last number.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Which of the following is treated as a text (label) entry rather than a numeric value in Excel by default?
questions.explanation: A name such as Kwame contains letters and so is stored as text, which Excel left-aligns by default. The entries 42, 3.14 and 100 are recognised as numbers, are right-aligned, and can be used directly in calculations.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: When a cell shows a row of hash marks such as ##### instead of a number, what does this usually mean?
options: The column is too narrow to display the value
The formula has a typing error
questions.explanation: A run of hash marks means the value is correct but the column is too narrow to show it, so Excel hides the digits rather than display them partly. Widening the column (or double-clicking the column border to auto-fit) reveals the number. It is not an error in the data itself.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: To add the contents of one cell to the contents of another using a formula, what must you type at the very start of the cell?
questions.explanation: Every formula in Excel must begin with an equals sign, for example =A1+B1. The equals sign tells Excel to calculate rather than store literal text. Without it, Excel would just keep the characters you typed as a label.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: A new blank Excel workbook is saved with which default file extension in modern versions of Excel?
questions.explanation: Modern Excel workbooks are saved with the .xlsx extension by default. The .docx extension belongs to Word documents, .txt is plain text, and .pptx is PowerPoint. The leading letters xls signal an Excel spreadsheet file.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Which statement best describes the relationship between a workbook and a worksheet in Excel?
options: They are two words for exactly the same thing
A worksheet can contain many workbooks
A worksheet is larger than a workbook
A workbook can contain many worksheets
questions.explanation: The workbook is the whole file, and inside it you can have several worksheets shown as tabs along the bottom. The relationship is one workbook holding many worksheets, not the reverse, and the two terms are not interchangeable.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible
